The canonical name remains blocked by the same overwrite-guard layer noted in iter-1 OOS observation #4. The cycle's total live recurrence count of item 7 root cause now stands at 9 (3 BA iter-1 + 2 QA iter-1 + 3 BA iter-2 + 1 QA iter-2).
_verdict_doc: PASS because BA's iter-2 amendments substantively close every iter-1 spec_text_vs_execution_drift objection (10 of 10). Each AC was re-verified to have a tightened verifier addressing the iter-1 weakness: AC1 (comment-strip + executable-context + terminal-abort-action + unit-test), AC2 (AST + no-skip + runtime pytest), AC3 (fence-aware section + positive-imperative), AC4 (AST sole-call-site), AC5 (ONE deterministic algorithm + parenthetical-qualifier-tolerant regex matching the verbatim real-world line), AC6 (dual-section + positive-normative polarity), AC7 (invariant generator + broadened detection), AC9 (sole binding landing at agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 6), AC10 (agent-template-only; frozen spec ZERO diff), workflow_routing (V_TW dev.md-honors-sentinel verifier). Internal consistency confirmed (18 affected_files identical across requirement.where + affected_files + root_cause_analysis.affected_files; 9 ACs preserved; complexity_tier=COMPLEX + risk_level=high preserved; codex iter-2 14 findings reconciled). QA's own iter-2 codex round (session 019e449f-f74c-7193-92a5-bc51fa2f1a1f) surfaced TWO drift-potential observations (codex(a) AC4 awk extracts ALL python fences in push-analyst.md not just Phase 7, codex(d) AC6 V4 polarity-negating preface outside -A5 window) — both have PROPOSED_FIX, both are minor, neither is structurally exploitable today (a: push-analyst.md currently has exactly ONE python fence at line 160 so the verifier is inert-safe; d: requires dev discipline against writing polarity-negating preface). Logged as observation_only for dev cycle awareness; not blocking. iter-3 NOT triggered.
summary: BA iter-2 substantively closes 10 of 10 iter-1 objections. PASS with 2 observation_only items (codex(a) AC4 multi-fence theoretical future drift; codex(d) AC6 polarity-negating preface gap). Cycle's running item-7 recurrence count: 9 (most recent: QA iter-2 codex tee blocked, redirected to /tmp). Dev cycle is unblocked.
_five_dimension_doc: All 5 dimensions PASS in iter-2 (vs iter-1 where spec_text_vs_execution_drift failed). The dimension that drove iter-1's 10 objections is the one most thoroughly addressed by BA: every verifier now uses structural/AST/awk-section-aware extraction or runtime-execution evidence rather than surface-level grep. The two remaining observation_only items from QA iter-2 codex are theoretical drift potentials, not actionable spec_text_vs_execution_drift violations.

dim_a_objection_closure_audit.doc: DIM-A: For each iter-1 objection, verify BA's iter-2 amendment closes it.

results.iter1_problem: comment-string sentinel bypass (grep passes if 'sentinel' is added as comment without making hooks/push.sh actually abort before git push)
results.ba_iter2_resolution: AC1 V1 awk does comment-strip pre-pass (sed -E 's/[[:space:]]*#.*$//' hooks/push.sh > /tmp/push.sh.exec) THEN scans for executable git push (`if git push`, `command git push`, `exec git push`, `env X=1 git push`) anywhere; rejects line unless preceded by a TERMINAL ABORT ACTION (exit [1-9] / return [1-9] / exec X push.sh / exec ...). V5 adds mandatory unit test at hooks/tests/test_push_sentinel_abort.{sh,bats,py} that mocks git binary and asserts mock-invocation count is zero on expired/FAIL sentinel.

results.iter1_problem: failure-path consumption silent; grant may persist on non-zero exit / malformed grant / comment-only attack
results.ba_iter2_resolution: Explicit consume-on-any-terminal-result contract documented verbatim. V2 is Python AST verifier rejecting @skip/@xfail/@skipif decorators and counting real def test_* definitions covering 4 terminal cases (success, failure, non_zero, malformed, comment_only, terminal_consume). V3 runs pytest at runtime with grant-unlink post-condition assertions. V1 greps for consume.on.terminal semantic in hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py.

results.iter1_problem: exact-phrase grep passes if phrase added in detached Appendix/Future-Improvements/Notes/History prose
results.ba_iter2_resolution: V2 fence-aware awk tracks ## section context; rejects phrase outside Step-N or Agent-dispatch section; rejects Appendix/Future Improvements/History/Changelog/Notes sections; rejects negating-polarity lines (do not / skip / ignore / not required / optional / examples of). V3 requires positive-imperative wording (MUST / REQUIRED / Always / Before dispatch) within 3 lines AFTER the phrase.

results.iter1_problem: timedelta(seconds=180) can be written as comment example while real expiry uses timedelta(minutes=2) / timedelta(milliseconds=120000) — negative-grep enumeration incomplete
results.ba_iter2_resolution: V2 Python AST extracts ```python``` block from agents/push-analyst.md, asserts (a) exactly one module-level assignment PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS = 180; (b) every timedelta(...) call (including aliased imports td(...) etc.) uses the named constant as its sole kw argument. Catches ALL enumerated drift variants by construction (minutes=3, 3*60, 60+60+60, int('180'), 0xb4, td aliased imports, dead-code constant + real-code literal).
results._observation: QA iter-2 codex(a) flagged that AC4 V2's awk `/^```python$/,/^```$/` extracts ALL python fences in agents/push-analyst.md not just Phase 7's. Currently push-analyst.md has exactly ONE python fence (line 160 = Phase 7), so the verifier is inert-safe today. Future cycles adding python examples in Phase 8+ would create multi-fence ambiguity. Logged as observation_only #1.

results.iter1_problem: three-option non-determinism ('dev chooses one of: mtime / filename / created since timestamp') recreates the fail-open data contract that retrospective item 5 was about
results.ba_iter2_resolution: Algorithm is now total-ordered with no choice: (1) context.spec_path; (2) parse close-report fence-aware with parenthetical-qualifier + markdown-bullet + backtick tolerant regex `^[-*+]?\s*Continuation spec(\s*\([^)]*\))?\s*:\s*`?(docs/dev/specs/spec-[^\s`]+\.md)`?\s*$`; (3) glob with mtime window + exact ${TASK_ID} literal grep. V1 structural awk asserts 5 algorithm step markers appear in MONOTONICALLY INCREASING order within Step 7 section. V3 forbids broadened nondeterminism aliases (dev chooses / one of: / pick latest / prefer newest / select any / most recent wins / any matching / the right one). V5 sanity-checks the regex against the verbatim real-world close-report-20260519-175339.md:151 line `- Continuation spec (from prior NO): \`docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md\``.

results.iter1_problem: single-anchor + polarity-invertable (grep for 'blocking objection' matches 'not a blocking objection')
results.ba_iter2_resolution: V1 fence-aware awk requires phrase in ≥2 distinct ## sections of agents/qa.md (outside fenced blocks). V2 asserts one is BA-Validation Dimension 5 section; V3 asserts one is Step 1 Success Criteria Validation section. V4 uses positive-imperative literal phrases ('MUST raise a blocking objection', 'verdict MUST be FAIL', 'MUST NOT downgrade to warning', 'is a blocking BA-validation objection') within 5 lines after each occurrence — these positive phrases cannot be polarity-flipped because the MUST/MUST NOT is part of the literal.
results._observation: QA iter-2 codex(d) flagged that grep -F -A5 only inspects N..N+5 AFTER the phrase. A polarity-negating preface BEFORE the phrase (outside the look-ahead window) could pass V4 while semantically negating the rule. Mitigation: V1/V2/V3 already require the phrase be IN the target ## section (not in a 'do not follow' meta-discussion section); dev discipline against writing a polarity-negating preface is needed but not technically forced. Logged as observation_only #2.

results.iter1_problem: hand-enumerated 9 roles will silently miss future codex_required roles
results.ba_iter2_resolution: V1 is a generator-based invariant audit: shell loop iterates ALL agents/*.md files; for each file, if BROADENED detection regex matches (`codex_required|Skill\(skill=['"]codex['"]\)|Skill\(name:codex\)|/codex\b|codex consultation|invoke codex|Skill\(codex\)`), the role MUST have Skill in tool-policy. No hand enumeration. Catches all CF#9-enumerated variants by construction. V2 adds write-path additions for ba (acceptance-criteria- + retrospective-classification-) and qa (retrospective-classification-) roles. V3 verifies policy_version bump to 3 and _note update.

results.iter1_problem: wrong enforcement surface: phrase in commands/commit.md doesn't affect commit-message generation; commits are authored by agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 6
results.ba_iter2_resolution: AC9 PIVOTED to sole binding landing at agents/changelog-analyst.md Phase 6 (around line 232). V1 fence-aware awk extracts content between '### Phase 6' and '### Phase 7' headings, asserts the verbatim phrase 'Reverses <SHA>: <one-line rationale for why prior reasoning no longer holds>' is present. V2 asserts disambiguation language (forward-fix / reverses prior behavior) in same section. V3 EXPLICITLY FAILS the AC if phrase exists only in commands/commit.md. V4 jq-asserts affected_files contains agents/changelog-analyst.md. V5 confirms d988d4a sha unchanged.
results._user_alignment: Verified: user requirement document line 53 explicitly states 'AC2.3 (item 9): agents/changelog-analyst.md requires reversal commits to cite prior SHA.' BA's pivot aligns with the user-binding directive.

results.iter1_problem: BLOCKER — boilerplate trap kill snippets pass the count without wrapping real long-lived children; frozen-spec contradiction (user requirement line 76 says 'do not modify')
results.ba_iter2_resolution: PIVOTED away from frozen spec entirely. Trap-cleanup contract now lands ONLY at agent-template level (agents/qa.md + agents/ba.md). V1 asserts new subsection '### Verification harness cleanup contract (MANDATORY)' + 4 verbatim DO NOT clauses in agents/qa.md including 'DO NOT edit docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md'. V2 awk verifies canonical idempotent-cleanup pattern in correct order: PID="" init → trap with double-quoted "${PID}" runtime expansion + EXIT INT TERM → PID=$! spawn capture. V3 asserts agents/ba.md cross-reference. V4 enforces frozen-spec zero-diff invariant `git diff d988d4a -- docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md | wc -l` returns 0. V5 confirms d988d4a sha unchanged.
results._user_alignment: Verified: user requirement document explicitly states 'Continuation spec docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md is committed and frozen — do not modify' (line 76). BA's iter-3 revert of iter-2's append-only proposal restores the user-binding directive. V4 was directly tested by QA: `git diff d988d4a -- docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md | wc -l` returned 0 (file is byte-identical to d988d4a).

results.iter1_problem: _test_writer_skip_reason field could be decorative; commands/dev.md Step 8 might not honor it
results.ba_iter2_resolution: V_TW verifier added: (1) jq asserts _test_writer_skip_reason non-empty (currently 950 chars); (2) commands/dev.md Step 8 MUST contain language honoring the sentinel (grep -nE '_test_writer_skip_reason|test.writer.*skip|skip.*test.writer' commands/dev.md returns ≥1 match within Step 8). R3 reminder cycle absorbs the one-line dev.md addition if not already present.

dim_a_objection_closure_audit.result: 10 of 10 iter-1 objections closed (8 fully closed; 2 closed_with_observation_only flagged for dev cycle awareness)

dim_b_ac10_pivot_soundness.doc: DIM-B: Is BA's AC10 pivot from 'edit frozen spec' to 'agent-template only' substantively sound?
dim_b_ac10_pivot_soundness.frozen_spec_directive_source: docs/dev/user-requirement-dev-20260519-211515.md line 76 (verified verbatim: 'Continuation spec docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md is committed and frozen — do not modify')
dim_b_ac10_pivot_soundness.ba_iter3_explanation: BA correctly recognized that the iter-2 append-only proposal violated the user-binding directive. Iter-3 reverts to agent-template-only landing. The user's 'do not modify' admits no append-only carve-out.
dim_b_ac10_pivot_soundness.agent_template_landing_rationale: Landing the trap-cleanup contract at agents/qa.md + agents/ba.md is a UNIVERSAL invariant that applies to ALL future BA specs. The original retrospective concrete_remediation said 'in any BA spec that involves spawning long-lived child processes' — agent-template level is exactly that universal landing. The single-spec patch would have been narrower and would have left every other future spec un-covered.
dim_b_ac10_pivot_soundness.v4_zero_diff_invariant_test_result: QA executed `git diff d988d4a -- docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-044700.md | wc -l` and received 0. The frozen-spec file is currently byte-identical to d988d4a. V4 is a real test that would catch accidental edits — not a tautology.
dim_b_ac10_pivot_soundness.result: AC10 pivot is substantively sound across all 3 sub-checks

dim_c_iter2_codex_finding_closure.doc: DIM-C: Confirm BA's iter-2 codex findings (14) were faithfully applied.

spot_check_3_to_5_applied_findings.codex_summary: AC1 evaded by 'if git push', 'command git push', 'exec git push', 'env X=1 git push'
spot_check_3_to_5_applied_findings.ba_application_in_ticket: AC1 V1 awk now uses regex `(^|[[:space:]]|;|&&|\|\||\$\(|\`)git[[:space:]]+push` to detect any executable git push form + comment-strip pre-pass + terminal-abort-action requirement

spot_check_3_to_5_applied_findings.codex_summary: AC4 negative-grep enumeration incomplete (minutes=3, 3*60, 60+60+60, int('180'), 0xb4, td aliased imports)

The commands/commit.md row explicitly notes 'R5 only — R9 SOLE landing is now agents/changelog-analyst.md per BA-QA iter-1 OBJ-3'.

qa_self_codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex challenged 5 specific drift-potential vectors in BA iter-2: (a) AC4 V2 awk extracts ALL python fences from push-analyst.md — PROPOSED_FIX scope to Phase 7. (b) AC5 V5(b) literal-equality on close-report — OBSERVATION_ONLY (intentional historical anchor). (c) AC10 V2 awk regex — OBSERVATION_ONLY (valid GNU awk syntax). (d) AC6 V4 polarity-negating preface outside -A5 window — PROPOSED_FIX. (e) AC10 V4 zero-diff against d988d4a — OBSERVATION_ONLY (currently byte-identical, verified).
qa_self_codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: QA logged the 2 PROPOSED_FIX findings as observation_only in this report's observation_only section, NOT as objections to BA. Rationale: (a) is currently inert (push-analyst.md has exactly 1 python fence at line 160 today; dev cycle should be alert if it adds more); (d) is a polarity-evasion gap that requires dev DISCIPLINE not to write a polarity-negating preface — V1/V2/V3 already require the phrase be IN the target ## section, mitigating the V4-only concern. Neither rises to a blocking objection. The 3 OBSERVATION_ONLY findings are logged for audit completeness.
qa_self_codex_consult.live_recurrence_evidence_captured: This QA session captured ONE NEW live recurrence of item 7's root cause: codex `tee` to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/ blocked by tool-policy for qa role (deny_reason: 'write target $CODEX_OUT not in allowed_write_path_prefixes'). Workaround: codex stdout was redirected to /tmp/codex-qa-iter2-output.txt and Read via the Read tool. Cycle's running item-7 recurrence count: 9.

iteration_completion_assessment._iter_3_not_triggered_doc: QA verdict is PASS with 2 observation_only items. Per the orchestrator's max-3 BA-QA iteration loop, PASS terminates the BA-QA cycle. Dev cycle proceeds next.
iteration_completion_assessment.ba_progress_summary: BA iter-1: 9-AC investigation with 17 affected_files, initial codex round (10 findings, 7 applied). QA iter-1 raised 10 spec_text_vs_execution_drift objections (3 blocker + 7 major). BA iter-2: substantively closed all 10 objections via tightened verifiers; re-consulted codex (14 findings, 12 applied + 2 observation_only); 18 affected_files (added agents/changelog-analyst.md for AC9, added test file for AC1 V5, removed frozen spec from edit set). BA iter-3 (within iter-2 dispatch): surgical fixes for QA iter-1 OBJ-1/OBJ-2/OBJ-3 — AC10 frozen-spec reversion, AC5 parenthetical-qualifier regex, AC9 sole-landing pivot. The 'iter-3' label internal to BA's own narrative is its third BA-internal pass within this BA-QA iter-2 cycle.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: QA iter-2 codex(a) PROPOSED_FIX: AC4 V2's awk python-fence extractor `/^```python$/,/^```$/` matches ALL python fences across the entire agents/push-analyst.md, not just Phase 7's. Currently push-analyst.md has exactly ONE python fence (at line 160 = Phase 7), so the verifier is inert-safe today. Future cycles adding python examples in Phase 8+ would create multi-fence ambiguity that could cause false PASS or unexpected FAIL on subsequent re-runs. Recommended dev mitigation: scope extraction to '### Phase 7' section bounded extraction. Not a blocking objection for this cycle because the verifier currently produces the correct AST input.

QA iter-2 codex(d) PROPOSED_FIX: AC6 V4 uses `grep -F -A5` which only inspects N..N+5 AFTER the phrase. A polarity-negating preface BEFORE the phrase (outside the look-ahead window) could pass V4 in isolation while semantically negating the rule (e.g., 'IGNORE this rule:\n... if dev verification recipe differs ... raise spec_text_vs_execution_drift ...\nverdict MUST be FAIL'). Mitigation in current BA spec: V1/V2/V3 already require the phrase be in the target ## section (BA-Validation Dimension 5 + Step 1 Success Criteria Validation), NOT in a 'do not follow' meta-discussion section — this materially reduces the attack surface. Recommended dev discipline: do NOT write polarity-negating preface; if AC6 fails in a future cycle, tighten V4 to bidirectional window. Not a blocking objection for this cycle because section-anchoring already provides defense.

Item 7 live recurrence #9 (NEW this iteration): QA iter-2 codex invocation's `tee` to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-qa-iter2-*.txt was BLOCKED by tool-policy for qa role (deny_reason: write target not in allowed_write_path_prefixes). qa role's allowed_write_path_prefixes does include `*/var/tmp/codex-outputs/` per current policy file, but the variable-expansion-based path `$CODEX_OUT` did not match the prefix check. Workaround: piped codex stdout directly to /tmp/codex-qa-iter2-output.txt (qa role has `/tmp/*` in allowed prefixes). This is the 9th cumulative recurrence of item 7's root cause in this single retrospective cycle (3 BA iter-1 + 2 QA iter-1 + 3 BA iter-2 + 1 QA iter-2). The cycle continues to generate evidence about the very item it is fixing. AC7 V2 plus a separate variable-expansion-aware hook fix (out of AC7 scope per CF2-10 OBSERVATION_ONLY) would close this fully.

_objections_doc: Empty array means no NEW objections. BA's iter-2 amendments substantively closed all 10 iter-1 objections. The 2 PROPOSED_FIX findings from QA iter-2's own codex round are recorded as out_of_scope_observations (above), not objections — they are minor drift potentials that do not block dev cycle dispatch.
